privacy_policy: STRICT NO-TRAINING by default. OpenRouter calls enforce this via provider.data_collection=deny (ZDR — zero data retention, no training). Subscription models (Kimi, GLM, Claude) operate under enterprise/API terms that exclude session training. Never add a model without documenting its no_training_source. DOCUMENTED EXCEPTION: Sakana Fugu (sakana_*) TRAINS on API prompts by default — no-training applies only after the user flips the console opt-out (console.sakana.ai); it is wired in as an explicit, flagged non-ZDR route (no_training=false in its tier-2 entry, surfaced by sakana_status and or_status) and is never auto-routed. Exa (exa_*) is a web-search/retrieval API, not a generative train-on-prompts LLM.
note: Re-run update_models.py to refresh. Only no-training / ZDR-eligible models included.

kimi.note: Prefer these first — subscription cost is zero marginal per token. General tools default to K2.7 (latest); kimi_analyze uses kimi-for-coding (rolling coding alias, currently K2.7). Override per call with the tools' model= arg (kimi_ask/kimi_swarm). Endpoint is reachable only with a coding-agent User-Agent (KimiCLI/x; set via KIMI_USER_AGENT).

glm_coding_plan.note: z.ai GLM Coding Plan — flat-rate subscription. No marginal $ within plan caps. Use as alternative to Kimi for second-opinion runs or to spread load.
glm_coding_plan.no_training_source: z.ai commercial API / Coding Plan terms — paid subscription, sessions not used for training.

claude_host.subscription: Max (current host Claude generation — Fable 5 / Opus 4.8 era; see host-model policy in global CLAUDE.md)
claude_host.note: The host IS Claude with a Max subscription. For tasks Claude handles well natively — writing, reasoning, coding, analysis, Q&A — redirect back to the host when it has token headroom. Only offload to OR when: (a) host signals token pressure, (b) task is genuinely outside Claude's strengths (Chinese text, 2M context, image generation), or (c) host explicitly requests offload.

image_generation_models.name: Flux 1.1 Pro
image_generation_models.best_for: Cinematic YouTube covers, stylized art, video thumbnail backgrounds, high-detail visuals
image_generation_models.no_training_source: OpenRouter ZDR: provider.data_collection=deny routes exclusively to zero-data-retention providers (no storage, no training). See openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/zdr

image_generation_models.name: Flux Schnell
image_generation_models.best_for: Rapid iteration on visual concepts, cheap bulk generation for storyboarding
image_generation_models.avoid_for: Final production images (use Flux 1.1 Pro or DALL-E 3)
image_generation_models.no_training_source: OpenRouter ZDR: provider.data_collection=deny routes exclusively to zero-data-retention providers (no storage, no training). See openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/zdr

image_generation_models.name: Ideogram V2
image_generation_models.best_for: YouTube title cards with text, logos, infographics — best text rendering of any image model
image_generation_models.avoid_for: Pure photorealism without text (DALL-E 3 or Flux better there)
image_generation_models.no_training_source: OpenRouter ZDR: provider.data_collection=deny routes exclusively to zero-data-retention providers (no storage, no training). See openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/zdr

image_generation_models.name: Stable Diffusion XL
image_generation_models.best_for: Cheap bulk image generation, custom fine-tuned styles, storyboard frames
image_generation_models.avoid_for: When quality > cost is the priority
image_generation_models.no_training_source: OpenRouter ZDR: provider.data_collection=deny routes exclusively to zero-data-retention providers (no storage, no training). See openrouter.ai/docs/guides/features/zdr

openrouter_models.name: DeepSeek V4 Pro
openrouter_models.best_for: Complex reasoning, math proofs, code when Kimi subscription unavailable
openrouter_models.no_training_source: DeepSeek commercial API terms + OpenRouter ZDR

openrouter_models.name: DeepSeek V4 Flash
openrouter_models.best_for: Fast cheap reasoning, batch tasks, time-sensitive queries on budget
openrouter_models.avoid_for: Deep multi-step reasoning where accuracy matters most
openrouter_models.no_training_source: DeepSeek commercial API terms + OpenRouter ZDR

openrouter_models.name: Claude Opus 4.7
openrouter_models.never_via_or_reason: Host IS Claude Code with Max subscription — use natively, paying OR would be double-billing
openrouter_models.best_for: Complex reasoning, long-form writing, nuanced analysis requiring judgment
openrouter_models.avoid_for: NEVER call via OpenRouter — use the host Claude natively (already covered by the Max subscription; calling via OR would double-charge).
openrouter_models.no_training_source: Anthropic API policy: does not train on API traffic. See anthropic.com/legal/privacy + OpenRouter ZDR

openrouter_models.name: MiMo-V2.5-Pro
openrouter_models.best_for: #1 by OpenRouter usage, strong general-purpose tasks, good price/performance
openrouter_models.no_training_source: OpenRouter ZDR (provider.data_collection=deny) — verify MiMo commercial terms

openrouter_models.name: MiMo-V2-Pro
openrouter_models.best_for: Strong reasoning and math tasks at a reasonable price point
openrouter_models.no_training_source: OpenRouter ZDR (provider.data_collection=deny) — verify MiMo commercial terms

openrouter_models.name: Qwen3.5 Plus
openrouter_models.best_for: Efficient reasoning at low cost, good multilingual support, 1M context
openrouter_models.avoid_for: Frontier tasks requiring strongest available model
openrouter_models.no_training_source: Alibaba Cloud API terms exclude training on API calls + OpenRouter ZDR

openrouter_models.name: Kimi K2.6 (OpenRouter — reference only)
openrouter_models.never_via_or_reason: Full Moonshot/Kimi flat-rate subscription — ALL moonshotai/* models have no marginal $ via KIMI_API_KEY. Never pay OR per-token for these.
openrouter_models.best_for: Use kimi_ask, kimi_analyze, kimi_swarm, kimi_batch via subscription (flat-rate; no marginal $)
openrouter_models.avoid_for: NEVER call via OpenRouter — all moonshotai/* models are blocked by _NEVER_VIA_OR guard
openrouter_models.no_training_source: Moonshot AI commercial subscription: API sessions not used for training (enterprise/API tier). Direct KIMI_API_KEY — not via OpenRouter.

openrouter_models.name: MiniMax M2.7
openrouter_models.best_for: Creative writing, storytelling, multimodal tasks — specialized and cheap
openrouter_models.avoid_for: Hard reasoning tasks, >205K context, code analysis

openrouter_models.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash
openrouter_models.best_for: Fast cheap multimodal tasks, large context at low cost, Google ecosystem
openrouter_models.avoid_for: Deep reasoning tasks, complex multi-step planning
openrouter_models.no_training_source: Google Cloud API terms: customer data not used for training + OpenRouter ZDR

openrouter_models.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
openrouter_models.best_for: Frontier reasoning / LLM-as-judge (top GPQA Diamond 94.3, HLE 44.4). Reached only by explicit model id — NOT wired into any profile/task_hint auto-route. Purpose: FLO gated audit voice (jojo-skills feedback-loop-optimization, v1.9.4), per-token, dormant by default.
openrouter_models.avoid_for: Routine cheap tasks (use Flash). Voting FLO judge before a truth-RMSE calibration probe (audit-only until probed — FLO invariant I3).
openrouter_models.no_training_source: Google paid Gemini API / Vertex: customer prompts+responses not used for training. OpenRouter ZDR (provider.data_collection=deny) routes only to a zero-retention Gemini endpoint and FAILS CLOSED if none is approved — this is the 'gated on approval' safety property. Verify the exact OpenRouter model id + that a ZDR Gemini provider exists via or_status / update_models.py --check-zdr when the key is provisioned.

profiles._comment: 5-tier transversal quality/cost profiles. Profile determines the default model when no specialist capability override applies. Specialist overrides (chinese, creative, eu_sovereignty) take precedence regardless of profile level. Subscription tiers (kimi subscription, host Claude) always win over per-token OR when task is in their domain — both are flat-rate paid subs, not free, but have no marginal $ within plan caps.

eco.description: Cheapest capable model — fast, good-enough quality for high-volume or low-stakes tasks
eco.note: Specialized tasks still use their specialist model regardless of eco setting. Eco only controls the default for general/unspecialized tasks.

mid.description: Balanced quality/cost — solid reasoning at a modest price
mid.note: Qwen3.5-plus: $0.30/M input, 1M context, efficient balanced reasoning. MiniMax M2.7 as fallback for creative-skewed tasks.

intel.description: Best reasoning, cost-aware — deep analysis and multi-step logic without paying for branding
intel.note: DeepSeek V4 Pro: 80.6% SWE-bench, $0.435/M in, 1.05M ctx. Best cost-adjusted reasoning model available via OR.

max.description: Strongest available general model — no cost constraint, maximum quality
max.note: MiMo-V2.5-Pro: #1 by OpenRouter usage, strong general intelligence. For reasoning-heavy max tasks: deepseek-v4-pro (80.6% SWE-bench at 43% the cost).

research.description: Long-context synthesis and deep analysis — prioritize models with ≥500K context
research.note: For coding/codebase analysis: use kimi_analyze (subscription, no marginal $, optimized). For very long documents (up to 1.05M tokens): deepseek-v4-pro. Qwen3.5-plus (1M ctx) as fallback. Models with <500K context are incompatible with this profile's intent.

coding.note: Kimi K2.6 subscription has no marginal $ — always prefer for coding

analysis.note: kimi_analyze handles full codebase ingestion — flat-rate sub, no marginal $

creative.note: MiniMax specialized for creative writing. anthropic/* excluded — use host Claude natively (already covered by the Max sub; OR would double-charge).

ultra_long_context.note: xAI/Grok banned. DeepSeek V4 Pro (1.05M ctx) is the best available long-context model. Qwen3.5-plus and Gemini Flash also have 1M+ context.

reasoning.note: Kimi handles most reasoning; DeepSeek V4 Pro for when Kimi falls short

premium.note: MiMo-V2.5-Pro is #1 by OR usage, strong frontier general capability. anthropic/* excluded — use host Claude natively (already on the Max sub; OR would double-charge). openai/* and x-ai/* banned.

fast_cheap.note: All under $0.30/M input — good for high-volume or low-stakes tasks

agentic.note: kimi_swarm uses Kimi's native swarm — flat-rate sub, no marginal $, optimized for multi-step

arithmetic_simple.note: Simple tasks do NOT warrant premium per-token models — Kimi sub has no marginal $ and is sufficient

image_generation.note: Image generation is the one category where Claude host cannot self-handle. Always route to OR image models. openai/dall-e-3 excluded (OpenAI banned).

host_self_handle.message: Your native Claude models (current host generation on your Max subscription — see host-model policy in global CLAUDE.md) are well-suited for this task. Handle it directly — no need to offload. Only call this MCP when you are token-constrained or the task needs a capability outside Claude's strengths (Chinese text, 2M context, image generation).
host_self_handle.override: If caller explicitly sets host_token_pressure=true, skip this check and route to OR/Kimi.

routing_config._comment: Defines the three-tier model access hierarchy. Edit this to add new subscriptions or change what the calling host can access natively. server.py reads this at startup — no code changes needed for new API key subscriptions.

tier_1_host_native.description: Models the calling host (Claude Code) can use natively under its Max subscription. Routing to these via OpenRouter would double-charge (paying OR per-token for capacity already covered by the sub). The MCP redirects back to the host for these tasks.
tier_1_host_native.note: Host is Claude Code with Max subscription (current generation — Fable 5 / Opus 4.8 era; see host-model policy in global CLAUDE.md). Add prefix 'google/' here if you get a Gemini subscription in the future.

tier_2_subscription_api.description: Models accessible via our own API keys — NOT via OpenRouter. Flat-rate subscription (paid up front, no marginal $ within plan caps). Always prefer over OpenRouter's per-token paid calls.

entries.note: Full Moonshot AI subscription — ALL moonshotai/* models are covered. Add new kimi model IDs here when they launch.
z.ai GLM Coding Plan subscription — flat-rate sub at api.z.ai/api/coding/paas/v4. OpenAI-compatible. Add new GLM model IDs here as z.ai releases them.

entries.billing: per-token paid (fugu $1.50/$6.00 per M, fugu-ultra $5.00/$30.00 per M); Sakana subscription plans also exist
entries.no_training_source: OPT-OUT REQUIRED — NOT confirmed no-training. Sakana TRAINS on API prompts by default; the no-training guarantee holds only after flipping the console opt-out (console.sakana.ai → account/privacy). No confirmed zero-retention, and there is NO per-call ZDR switch (unlike OpenRouter). sakana_status surfaces this; treat as a non-ZDR route until opted out.
entries.note: Sakana Fugu — Fable-tier multi-agent frontier model that orchestrates a pool of LLMs behind one OpenAI-compatible endpoint (api.sakana.ai/v1). fugu = fast default; fugu-ultra = deep multi-step reasoning, 272K ctx. Reasoning effort high|xhigh via the effort= arg. Direct key → blocked from OR routing; never auto-routed.

Exa web search / retrieval API (api.exa.ai; x-api-key auth, NOT chat-completions). exa_search (type auto|fast|instant|deep-lite|deep|deep-reasoning, highlights/text, outputSchema synthesis), exa_answer (grounded answer + citations), exa_contents (extract known URLs). A search tool, not a generative LLM — listed with an empty-id prefix only to keep it out of OR routing and visible in or_status.

tier_2_subscription_api.note: Add new entries here when you get additional API subscriptions (e.g. DeepSeek direct API, Mistral subscription).

tier_3_openrouter.description: All remaining models — paid per token via OpenRouter. Only use when tier 1 and tier 2 cannot handle the task.

banned_providers._comment: Providers refused entirely, by user choice. Edit here to add/remove. Enforced in _or_chat and or_compare.
banned_providers.reason: User preference — OpenAI (openai/) and xAI (x-ai/) excluded from all routing
